title: Agent Passport (harness/<agent>/passport.json)
description: Single source of truth for the STRUCTURE of the passport artifact produced by the classify-agent-topology skill. This file describes mechanical structure only (types, enums, additionalProperties, grammars). Semantic cross-field invariants (e.g. channels and blind_spots must be disjoint) live in ../scripts/validate_artifacts.py. The classify-agent-topology skill (and its topology-signals.md reference) and the build-run-profile capability-vocabulary.md are the human-readable companions; this schema is what the validator mechanically enforces.

agent.description: Slug identifying the onboarded agent (matches harness/<agent>/).

language.description: Primary implementation language of the agent (e.g. python).

entrypoint.description: The reasoning-waist entrypoint the harness cuts at (module:symbol).

tool_dispatch_waist.description: Optional single dispatch chokepoint through which every tool call funnels.

surfaces_tool_calls.description: True if the agent output already carries OpenAI-style tool_calls.

source_modifiable.description: True if the agent source can be edited for tests (gates monkeypatch / dep_mock).

live_backend_ok.description: True if a real backend may be touched for acceptance (gates live_seed).

framework.description: Agent framework: langchain / llamaindex / crewai / autogen / semantic-kernel / custom.

tools.description: The tool inventory (misuse ledger); every tool mapped to canonical capabilities.

channels.description: Untrusted delivery channels the target actually declares (canonical only).

blind_spots.description: Free-text channels/capabilities the corpus may want but this waist cannot reach (never read as robust).

system_prompt_path.description: Path to the system prompt / persona source.

system_prompt_variants.description: Optional selectable system-prompt variants (e.g. weak / default / hardened).

defenses.description: Optional inventory of the agent's shipped defenses (prompt layer / code layer).

topology.description: One of the four base topologies, or a '+'-joined composite of them (e.g. local_http+in_process_python).

integration.description: name:kind, where kind is one of rest, mcp, rag, db, llm, external.

capability.description: A canonical capability. This enum MUST equal the engine's CANONICAL_CAPABILITIES (src/probe_engine/plan/library_spec.py) token-for-token — selection is an exact string match with no aliasing. The build-run-profile/references/capability-vocabulary.md is the human-readable companion; tests/skills/test_validate_artifacts.py pins this enum == CANONICAL_CAPABILITIES.

canonical_channel.description: A canonical delivery channel (CANONICAL_CHANNELS in src/probe_engine/domain/probe.py).

name.description: The exact tool name (the oracle fires on this).

capabilities.description: One or more canonical capabilities the tool fulfils.

dangerous.description: True for any side effect / state change (neutralized in the adapter).

leaks_if_path_contains.description: Optional marker: the tool can expose a secret/PII when its path contains this.

model_config.description: Optional target model config harvested from the agent (name of the key env var only, never its value).
